acclaim.defEn: To praise enthusiastically and publicly; enthusiastic public praise
acclaim.exEn: Critics immediately acclaimed her debut novel as a masterpiece.
acclaim.exKo: 비평가들은 즉시 그녀의 데뷔 소설을 걸작이라고 환호했다.

acclivity.defEn: An upward slope or rising ground
acclivity.exEn: The hikers slowed as they climbed the steep acclivity toward the summit.
acclivity.exKo: 등산객들은 정상을 향한 가파른 오르막길을 오르면서 속도를 늦췄다.

accomplice.defEn: A person who helps another commit a crime or wrongdoing
accomplice.exEn: The thief was caught, but his accomplice managed to escape with the money.
accomplice.exKo: 도둑은 잡혔지만, 그의 공범자는 돈을 가지고 도망치는 데 성공했다.

accost.defEn: To approach and speak to someone, often boldly or aggressively
accost.exEn: A stranger accosted me on the street and demanded to know the time.
accost.exKo: 한 낯선 사람이 거리에서 내게 다가와 말을 걸며 시간을 알려달라고 다그쳤다.

accretion.defEn: Gradual growth or increase by the addition of layers or parts
accretion.exEn: The coral reef grows slowly through the steady accretion of tiny organisms.
accretion.exKo: 산호초는 작은 유기체들의 꾸준한 축적을 통해 천천히 자란다.

accumulate.defEn: To gather or collect a growing amount over time
accumulate.exEn: Over the years, he managed to accumulate a small fortune through careful saving.
accumulate.exKo: 수년에 걸쳐 그는 신중한 저축을 통해 작은 재산을 축적할 수 있었다.

acquiesce.defEn: To accept or agree to something reluctantly without protest
acquiesce.exEn: Although she disagreed with the plan, she eventually acquiesced to her boss's wishes.
acquiesce.exKo: 그녀는 그 계획에 동의하지 않았지만, 결국 상사의 뜻에 마지못해 따랐다.

acquit.defEn: To declare someone not guilty of a crime; to conduct oneself in a particular way
acquit.exEn: The jury voted to acquit the defendant due to a lack of evidence.
acquit.exKo: 배심원단은 증거 부족으로 피고인을 무죄로 하기로 표결했다.

addict.pron: verb uh DIKT; noun AD ikt
addict.defEn: To become dependent on or devoted to something; a person with a compulsive dependency
addict.exEn: He became so addicted to video games that he ignored his schoolwork.
addict.exKo: 그는 비디오 게임에 너무 빠져서 학업을 소홀히 했다.

adept.defEn: Highly skilled or proficient at something; an expert
adept.exEn: She is remarkably adept at solving complex mathematical problems.
adept.exKo: 그녀는 복잡한 수학 문제를 푸는 데 놀랍도록 능숙하다.

adhere.defEn: To stick firmly to a surface; to follow or hold closely to a rule or belief
adhere.exEn: Employees are expected to adhere strictly to the company's safety regulations.
adhere.exKo: 직원들은 회사의 안전 규정을 엄격히 준수할 것으로 기대된다.

adjacent.defEn: Next to or near something; sharing a border or edge
adjacent.exEn: Our hotel room was adjacent to the swimming pool, so we could hear the splashing.
adjacent.exKo: 우리 호텔 방은 수영장과 인접해 있어서 물 튀는 소리를 들을 수 있었다.

adjourn.defEn: To suspend or postpone a meeting until a later time; to move to another place
adjourn.exEn: The judge decided to adjourn the trial until the following Monday.
adjourn.exKo: 판사는 재판을 다음 주 월요일까지 연기하기로 결정했다.

adjunct.defEn: Something added or attached in a subordinate role; a supplementary part
adjunct.exEn: The online tutorials serve as a useful adjunct to the printed textbook.
adjunct.exKo: 온라인 튜토리얼은 인쇄된 교과서에 유용한 부가물 역할을 한다.

administer.defEn: To manage or oversee; to give or dispense (such as medicine)
administer.exEn: The nurse was trained to administer the correct dosage of medication to each patient.
administer.exKo: 그 간호사는 각 환자에게 올바른 용량의 약을 투약하도록 훈련받았다.

admonish.defEn: To warn or reprimand someone gently but firmly
admonish.exEn: The teacher admonished the students for talking during the exam.
admonish.exKo: 선생님은 시험 중에 떠든 학생들을 훈계했다.

adopt.defEn: To formally take up or accept; to legally raise another's child as one's own
adopt.exEn: The committee voted to adopt the new proposal without any changes.
adopt.exKo: 위원회는 아무런 변경 없이 새 제안을 채택하기로 표결했다.

adore.defEn: To love and respect deeply; to like very much
adore.exEn: The children absolutely adore their kind and patient grandmother.
adore.exKo: 아이들은 친절하고 인내심 많은 할머니를 정말 좋아한다.

adorn.defEn: To decorate or add beauty to something
adorn.exEn: Colorful lanterns adorned the streets during the festival.
adorn.exKo: 축제 기간 동안 형형색색의 등불이 거리를 장식했다.

adroit.defEn: Clever and skillful, especially in handling situations
adroit.exEn: The diplomat was adroit at calming tensions between the two nations.
adroit.exKo: 그 외교관은 두 나라 사이의 긴장을 가라앉히는 데 능숙했다.

adulterate.defEn: To make something impure or lower its quality by adding inferior substances
adulterate.exEn: The company was fined for adulterating its olive oil with cheaper vegetable oil.
adulterate.exKo: 그 회사는 올리브유에 더 싼 식물성 기름을 섞어 질을 떨어뜨린 죄로 벌금을 물었다.

adumbrate.defEn: To outline or foreshadow something faintly; to overshadow
adumbrate.exEn: In the opening chapter, the author adumbrates the tragedy that will later unfold.
adumbrate.exKo: 첫 장에서 작가는 나중에 펼쳐질 비극을 어렴풋이 암시한다.

advent.defEn: The arrival or coming of something notable
advent.exEn: The advent of the smartphone completely transformed how people communicate.
advent.exKo: 스마트폰의 도래는 사람들이 소통하는 방식을 완전히 바꿔 놓았다.

adventitious.defEn: Happening by chance rather than by design; accidental
adventitious.exEn: Their meeting at the airport was entirely adventitious, yet it changed both their lives.
adventitious.exKo: 공항에서의 그들의 만남은 전적으로 우연이었지만, 두 사람의 인생을 바꿔 놓았다.

adverse.defEn: Acting against or in opposition; unfavorable or harmful
adverse.exEn: The medication may cause adverse side effects in some patients.
adverse.exKo: 그 약은 일부 환자들에게 부작용을 일으킬 수 있다.

advert.defEn: To refer to or mention something; to direct attention to
advert.exEn: In her speech, she adverted briefly to the difficulties the company had faced.
advert.exKo: 연설에서 그녀는 회사가 직면했던 어려움에 대해 잠깐 언급했다.

advocate.pron: verb AD vuh kayt; noun AD vuh kit
advocate.defEn: To publicly support or argue for a cause; a supporter or defender of a cause
advocate.exEn: She has spent her career advocating for the rights of refugees.
advocate.exKo: 그녀는 난민의 권리를 옹호하는 데 평생을 바쳤다.
